RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 415-A, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Jeff Vernetti, a Republican, represents Camden County (District 123) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first term in November of 2024.
Born in St. Louis, Vernetti graduated from Lafayette High School in 1995. Then in 1999 he graduated from Southeast Missouri State University with a B.S. in Mass Communications.
A local business owner and real estate developer, Vernetti has spearheaded numerous projects in the Lake of the Ozarks area. He co-founded and co-owns Ballparks National, a nationally recognized youth baseball and softball complex. During his tenure as General Manager, Ballparks National hosted over 5,000 games, drawing teams from 21 states and contributing millions to the local economy. The facility employs over 50 staff members and has received accolades, including Missouri’s Best Youth Sports Complex. Vernetti also co-owns LOZ Sports Training, the region’s premier youth sports facility, and Vernetti’s Italian Grocer, a specialty market offering St. Louis-style Italian fare.
Before relocating to the Lake area, Vernetti worked in St. Louis as a sports broadcaster and business owner, gaining experience in sales, marketing, and operations management. With a career spanning sales leadership roles across multiple industries, Vernetti brings extensive expertise in budget management, personnel oversight, and community development to his role in public service.
He is involved in a number of local organizations and is currently a member of the Camdenton Rotary Club, the Osage Beach Elks Club, and Calvary Chapel Lake of the Ozarks. Previously, he sat on the board of directors for Central Ozarks Medical Center (COMC), the Lake West Chamber of Commerce, and the Lake of the Ozarks Regional Economic Development Council.
Vernetti and his wife, Kelly, live in Camdenton and have two children, Dominick and Isabella.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Authorizes the conveyance of certain state property in Miller County
Modifies provisions related to land reclamation
Designates the "Police Officer Phylicia Carson Memorial Bridge" in Camden County
Adds a circuit judge to the 26th Judicial Circuit
Phases out the corporate income tax
Modifies provisions relating to income tax on private pensions
Establishes the "Entrepreneur Rights Act", exempting certain businesses from minimum wage increases and paid sick leave provisions
Prohibits the use of automated traffic enforcement systems and the use of automated license plate readers
Modifies provisions relating to enforcement of laws relating the use of electronic communication devices

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Modifies provisions relating to tax credits for sporting events
Modifies provisions relating to mail sent by state agencies
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Modifies the Tourism Supplemental Revenue Fund
Modifies provisions relating to the classification of certain residential real property used for short-term rentals
